description:
Trigger: caller constructs two Pkcs11Backend instances pointing at the same .so (e.g., one for a data object, one for a signing key in the same token).

Bad behavior: secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs:90-98 calls Pkcs11::new(&lib_path) and ctx.initialize(...) on every from_uri. PKCS#11 spec (PKCS11 v2.40 §5.4) says C_Initialize must be called once per process. The second Pkcs11Backend's initialize will return CKR_CRYPTOKI_ALREADY_INITIALIZED, which cryptoki may surface as an error — meaning the second from_uri fails on a perfectly valid URI when the first instance is still alive. This is a class of "works in tests, breaks in production" bug because tests typically construct a single backend.

Fix: cache Pkcs11 contexts by canonical lib path in a process-wide LazyLock<Mutex<HashMap<PathBuf, Arc<Pkcs11>>>>, return a clone of the existing Arc on subsequent from_uri calls for the same library. cryptoki's reference counting won't save us here — different Pkcs11::new() calls produce independent contexts.
title: C_Initialize called per Pkcs11Backend instance — second instance for same lib breaks
